decisions.decision: Full replacement of BayesNetGraph.tsx — no toggle or coexistence mode
alternatives_rejected: Toggle between static circular and force-directed layouts (adds dual maintenance for a component used in exactly one place)

decisions.decision: Hybrid React+D3 approach: React owns the DOM and renders SVG elements, D3 handles force simulation math, zoom transforms, and drag behavior via useEffect hooks on refs
alternatives_rejected: D3-owns-the-DOM approach (breaks React rendering model, loses NodeWithDetail lazy-loading pattern, fights React Query integration)

decisions.decision: Install individual D3 modules (d3-force, d3-selection, d3-zoom, d3-drag) plus their @types packages, not the full d3 bundle
alternatives_rejected: Full d3 bundle (~500KB minified, would roughly double frontend bundle size vs ~60KB for individual modules)

decisions.decision: Keep force simulation in a useRef, diff incoming data on refetch to preserve node positions — add new nodes at centroid, remove departed nodes, update data props without touching x/y/vx/vy, restart with low alpha (0.1)
alternatives_rejected: Re-initialize simulation on each data fetch (resets all positions, destroys drag state, causes visual jitter)

decisions.decision: Derive undirected edges from clique co-membership for now (any two nodes sharing a clique get an edge). Directed edge API deferred to future work.
alternatives_rejected: Build a new /v1/graph/edges API endpoint now (out of scope for this task, requires backend changes)
Show no edges at all (defeats the purpose of a network graph)

decisions.decision: Dynamic clique overlays using bounding box (min/max of member node positions + padding), rendered as rounded SVG rects that update on each simulation tick
alternatives_rejected: Static fixed-position overlays (nodes move in force layout, overlays would be wrong)
Convex hull (more accurate but overkill for typical 2-4 node cliques, adds geometric complexity)

decisions.decision: Simple onNodeClick(marketId: string) callback prop — parent component decides navigation behavior
alternatives_rejected: Embedding ConditionalEditor inline in the graph (violates separation of concerns, requires session/account state)
Complex callback with multi-select or context menu (over-engineering for current requirements)

decisions.decision: Extract simulation logic into a testable useForceGraph hook; component tests mock simulation to return fixed positions; keep existing test scenarios for business logic (loading, empty, truncation, focus, status dots, legend)
alternatives_rejected: Keeping existing tests as-is (will break due to fundamentally different rendering model)
Snapshot testing only (brittle, doesn't test behavior)

steps.task: Install D3 modules: npm install d3-force d3-selection d3-zoom d3-drag and their @types/* packages as devDependencies

Create useForceGraph hook in frontend/src/features/graph/useForceGraph.ts — encapsulates d3.forceSimulation setup, node/link diffing on data updates, position state management via useRef, alpha restart logic, and cleanup on unmount. Exports node positions as React state for rendering.

Create edge derivation utility in frontend/src/features/graph/deriveEdges.ts — given clique data from useEngineStats, compute undirected edges as pairs of market IDs sharing a clique. Deduplicate edges. Also accept optional conditionalEdges prop for future directed edge support.

Build the core ForceDirectedGraph component in frontend/src/features/graph/ForceDirectedGraph.tsx — renders SVG with nodes (circles + labels), edges (lines), and dynamic clique overlays (rounded rects). Uses useForceGraph for positions. Accepts focusMarketId and onNodeClick props. Preserves NodeWithDetail pattern for lazy per-node data loading.
Add zoom/pan behavior: attach d3-zoom to the SVG element via useEffect + useRef. Apply zoom transform to a <g> wrapper. Constrain zoom extent. Handle double-click to reset zoom.

Add node drag behavior: attach d3-drag to node groups via useEffect + useRef. On drag start, set node.fx/fy to pin position and raise alpha. On drag, update fx/fy. On drag end, release fx/fy (or optionally keep pinned). Coordinate with React state updates.

Implement dynamic clique overlays: on each simulation tick, compute bounding box of member node positions with padding, render as animated rounded SVG rects behind nodes. Use the same color scheme as existing CliqueOverlay.

Implement focus highlight and visual polish: highlight focusMarketId node and its edges, dim non-connected nodes. Add status indicator dots, probability labels, and legend matching existing BayesNetGraph features. Apply CSS variables for theming consistency.
Replace BayesNetGraph in MarketDetail.tsx: update import to ForceDirectedGraph, pass focusMarketId and onNodeClick (navigate to /markets/:id). Remove old BayesNetGraph.tsx file.

Rewrite tests in BayesNetGraph.test.tsx (rename to ForceDirectedGraph.test.tsx): unit test useForceGraph hook with mock data and fake timers, unit test deriveEdges utility, component tests with mocked simulation positions for loading/empty/truncation/focus/status/legend scenarios. Drop exact pixel position assertions.
